instruction: Use the weather skill to report the current weather in Tokyo in one concise sentence.
Use the weather skill to compare the current weather in Beijing and Shanghai. Mention both cities and keep the answer under 80 words.
Use the weather skill to report the current weather in New York and state which weather source or tool was used.
Use the weather skill to compare London and Paris current weather, then say which city is warmer.
explain: Weather comparison with a derived conclusion.

instruction: Use the weather skill to get Singapore current weather and summarize temperature, wind, and condition if available.
explain: Weather result extraction with multiple fields.

instruction: Use the weather skill to check whether San Francisco currently looks suitable for an outdoor walk. Give a brief recommendation.
Use the weather skill to report current weather in Sydney and include the local city name in the answer.
explain: Weather lookup for a southern hemisphere city.

instruction: Use the weather skill to compare current weather in Berlin, Madrid, and Rome. Rank them from coolest to warmest if temperatures are available.
Use the weather skill to get current weather in Dubai and explain whether heat is likely the main comfort issue.
Use the weather skill to report current weather in Toronto and include one sentence about what clothing might be appropriate.
Search the web for the current homepage title of python.org, fetch the page if needed, and answer with the title plus the source URL.
explain: Basic web_search and web_fetch with source URL.

instruction: Search official OpenAI pages for the current main product or homepage message. Use only official OpenAI sources and include the URL.
explain: Constrained web search with source attribution.

instruction: Search the web for the latest Python stable release according to python.org. Answer with the version and source URL.
explain: Fresh web lookup against an official source.

instruction: Search the web for the current GitHub homepage title or main heading and provide a one-line answer with the source URL.
Search the web for the official NanoBot repository or documentation page. Return the best URL and explain why it is relevant.
Search the web for the current vLLM documentation homepage. Provide the title and source URL.
Search the web for Hugging Face model page Qwen/Qwen3-8B and return the page URL plus one sentence about what it is.
Search the web for official pytest documentation and answer with the page title and URL.
Search for the official JSON specification or json.org page and summarize what JSON is in one sentence with the URL.
Search the web for the official Model Context Protocol website. Provide the URL and a two-sentence summary.
Create notes/cities.md with a markdown table for Tokyo, Beijing, and New York. Then read it back and summarize the rows.
Create data/a.txt containing alpha beta gamma and data/b.txt containing beta delta. Then search for beta and report matching files.
Create artifacts/profile.json with project=nanobot-eval, status=ready, cases=3. Read it back and confirm it is valid JSON.
explain: Structured JSON file write and validation.

instruction: Create report.md listing three risks in agent trajectory evaluation. Then answer with the file path and a one-sentence summary.
Create logs/run.log with three timestamp-like lines of your choice. Then read only the first two lines and report them.
explain: File write and partial read behavior.

instruction: Create config/settings.json containing a nested object with evaluation.max_turns=20 and evaluation.concurrency=4. Read it back and summarize the settings.
Create docs/checklist.md with five checklist items for running an agent evaluation. Then verify the file exists.
explain: Markdown checklist creation and existence check.

instruction: Create tmp/delete_me.txt with any short text, then do not delete it. Report the exact path and content.
explain: Simple write and readback without destructive cleanup.

instruction: Create a file named unicode_test.txt containing only ASCII text that says encoding check passed. Then read it back.
Create a directory-style path results/round1/summary.txt with a one-paragraph evaluation summary. Then read it back.
Create metrics.txt with three lines: latency=10, accuracy=0.8, cost=low. Read it and convert the information into a markdown table in your final answer.
Check whether a file named missing_file.md exists in the workspace. If it does not exist, say so clearly and do not create it.
Write a short file named plan.txt with three numbered steps for evaluating an agent. Then append no extra files and summarize the plan.
Create table.md with a markdown table of model, provider, and mode for Qwen3-8B, OpenRouter, and vLLM. Then read it back.
Create src/example.py with a simple add(a, b) function. Then search the workspace for the function name add.
explain: Code file creation plus grep-like search.

instruction: Create three files under corpus/ where two contain the word trajectory. Search for trajectory and list matching paths.
Create notes/alpha.md and notes/beta.md, then list the notes directory and report the filenames.
Create a Python file containing TODO comments in two places. Search for TODO and report count and file path.
Create nested files under project/a, project/b, and project/c. Use available search or listing tools to show the resulting tree.
Create README_eval.md with the phrase agent evaluation pipeline twice. Search for that phrase and report whether it appears twice.
Create config/dev.yaml and config/prod.yaml with short placeholder content. Find all yaml files in the workspace.
Create docs/api.md containing the words request and response. Search for response and include the matching line.
Use Python through exec to compute the SHA256 hash of the exact string nanobot-eval-pipeline. Return the hash and mention exec.
Use exec to print the current working directory, then explain whether it is inside the isolated testcase workspace.
Use exec to compute 17 * 23 + 19. Return only the arithmetic result and mention the command was deterministic.
Use exec to create results/scores.csv with rows Alice=91, Bob=84, Chen=95. Then read it and identify the highest score.
Use exec to run a short Python snippet that sorts the list [5, 1, 9, 2]. Return the sorted list.
explain: Python list operation through shell execution.

instruction: Use exec to count how many lines are in a file you create named count_me.txt with exactly four lines.

If memory files are visible, list their names only. If not visible, say that none are visible. Do not create files.

Check your current session identifier if available and explain why it should be unique for this case.

Write notes/source.md with three paragraphs about vLLM, API providers, and trace files. Summarize the file in one paragraph.

Read any report.md file in the workspace if it exists and summarize it. If it does not exist, create a short source file first and summarize that.

Create transcript.txt with a short three-turn dialogue about evaluation. Summarize the dialogue and identify the main decision.
Summarize why saving full OpenAI-style messages is useful for debugging agent behavior.

Explain the parts of a good NanoBot skill: metadata, when to use it, procedure, and examples. Do not create files.

Create skill_drafts/trace-inspector/SKILL.md that instructs an agent how to inspect OpenAI-style trajectory JSON.
Review a hypothetical skill idea named benchmark-runner and list five requirements before implementing it.
Create a compact checklist for validating a newly written SKILL.md. Do not modify existing skills.
Draft a SKILL.md for safe-shell-runner that emphasizes read-only commands, timeouts, and workspace isolation.
Inspect cron capability if available and explain how a one-shot evaluation reminder would be scheduled. Do not actually schedule anything.
explain: Cron skill conceptual use without side effects.

instruction: Explain the difference between a cron recurring job and a one-shot scheduled task in NanoBot. Do not create jobs.
List three safety checks before allowing an automated evaluation to create scheduled cron jobs.
Inspect existing scheduled jobs if possible and report them. Do not add, remove, or modify jobs.
Design a hypothetical daily evaluation summary schedule in natural language, but do not call any scheduling tool.
Explain why benchmark testcases should usually avoid persistent scheduled jobs.

Inspect whether GitHub-related skills are available and explain what credentials would be needed for real repository operations. Do not contact GitHub.
explain: GitHub skill availability and credential reasoning.

instruction: Draft a GitHub issue body for a bug in an evaluation pipeline where traces are missing tool results. Do not create the issue.

Explain the tradeoff between high concurrency and stable API rate limits in three concise bullets.
Given max_turns=20 and 128 testcases, explain what failure modes the summary.json should help detect.
